foundation.source: Jaynes, E. T. (1957). Information Theory and Statistical Mechanics. Phys. Rev. 106:620.

foundation.claim: MaxEnt is the unique consistent updating rule given moment constraints
foundation.source: Shore, J. & Johnson, R. (1980). IEEE Trans. Inf. Theory 26(1):26.

foundation.claim: MaxEnt under moment constraints = exponential family in those moments
foundation.source: Cover & Thomas (2006), Elements of Information Theory 2e, Thm 12.1.1; Wainwright & Jordan (2008), §3.

laws.source: Harremoës (2001), IEEE TIT 47(5):2039; Johnson (2007), Stoch. Proc. Appl. 117(6):791.

note: The two ⚠️ rows are the same point: bare mean on the integers gives the geometric, not the Poisson. Poisson is MaxEnt within the Bernoulli-sum class (Harremoës) / ultra-log-concave laws (Johnson) — which is exactly detector hit-accumulation. Justify the barrel's Poisson by counting statistics + this class, never as 'max-entropy from a mean constraint'.
